title: IC50 dose-response (384-well)
description: 384-well biochemical / enzyme-inhibition IC50 layout: background blank, vehicle (0% inhibition anchor), reference inhibitor (100% inhibition anchor), and a 20-point test-compound serial dilution for a 4-parameter curve fit.

payload.description: IC50 dose-response plate for a 384-well plate (16 rows A-P x 24 columns), for an enzyme-inhibition, receptor-binding, or other target-activity assay, scaled from the NCBI Assay Guidance Manual dose-response guidance (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK144065/). Keeps the two anchor controls a curve fit needs. Column 1 = background blank (no enzyme/substrate, or no cells). Column 2 = vehicle control (full activity, 0% inhibition anchor / top of the curve). Column 3 = reference inhibitor at a saturating dose (100% inhibition anchor / bottom of the curve). Columns 4-23 = a 20-point serial dilution of the test compound (typically 1:2 or 1:3), highest dose column 4 descending to column 23; each row A-P is an independent compound or a replicate. Compute % inhibition between the two anchor controls and fit % inhibition vs log[dose] with a 4-parameter logistic to read IC50.

region_labels.notes: No-enzyme / no-substrate (or no-cell) background. Subtract from every other well before normalizing.
Full assay + vehicle, no inhibitor. Maximum-signal / 0% inhibition anchor (top of the 4-parameter curve).
Saturating dose of a known inhibitor. Minimum-signal / 100% inhibition anchor (bottom of the curve).

Test-compound 20-point serial dilution (typically 1:2 or 1:3), highest dose in column 4 descending to column 23. Each row A-P is an independent compound or a replicate. Fit % inhibition vs log[dose] for IC50.
Spare column: a second reference, an extra replicate, or a medium border to limit edge effects.
